Lepechinia aurantiaca, commonly known as Mexican mountain mint, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lepechinia aurantiaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
